data.title: Travere Therapeutics: Buy Rating On Back Of Filspari Expansion Towards FSGS
data.description:
I'm starting coverage on Travere Therapeutics, Inc. (NASDAQ:TVTX) with a Buy Rating on its stock because I believe it has massive potential when it comes to its drug sparsentan. The first reason is because this endothelin and angiotensin II receptor antagonist has been approved to slow kidney function decline in patients with immunoglobulin A nephropathy [IgAN] who are at risk of disease progression. It is not just the mere fact that the company has been able to receive FDA approval of this drug, but the notion that it is seeing a massive spike increase in sales of it. Consider that U.S. net product revenues for Filspari have reached $71.9 million, which ends up being a year-over-year increases of 165%.

Why do I believe it is important to go over this biotech now? Well, the growth in net product revenues for this drug are good, but consider that there is potential for a major expansion opportunity. In essence, the company had submitted and the FDA had accepted a supplemental New Drug Application [sNDA] submission of Filspari [sparsentan] to treat patients with a rare kidney disorder known as focal segmental glomerulosclerosis [FSGS]. A Prescription Drug User Fee Act [PDUFA] date of January 13, 2026 was established in order to review by whether or not it should be approved to treat these patients. Here is the kicker, when the sNDA was first accepted, it was noted that the FDA was planning to hold an advisory committee meeting.

However, there recently was a huge update provided by Travere. It was notified by this U.S agency itself that such an advisory committee meeting would no longer be needed. The beauty of this company is that it holds the potential to have the first approved drug for the treatment of patients with FSGS. This doesn't even include the potential of its enzyme replacement therapy [ERT] Pegtibatinase [TVT-058], which is being developed to target patients with classical homocystinuria [HCU] in a phase 3 study to be known as Harmony. A late-stage trial of this caliber to target this group of patients is expected to start in 2026.

sNDA Provides Opportunity To Continue Massive Revenue Growth Of Filspari

As I noted above, Travere Therapeutics has already received FDA approval of its drug Filspari for the treatment of patients with immunoglobulin A nephropathy. Sales of this drug have been coming along very well and I will be going over such numbers in the "Financials" portion of this article. However, what I want to focus on specifically is the development of Filspari for the treatment of patients with a rare kidney disorder known as focal segmental glomerulosclerosis. That's because the company was not only able to submit the sNDA of this drug for the targeting of these patients, but the FDA has already established a specific PDUFA date.

The reason for the sNDA of Filspari being filed with the FDA is because of positive results from two studies that were completed. One was the phase 2 DUET study and the other one was the phase 3 DUPLEX trial. Before going over the data from either of these studies, plus any catalysts to come about from this specific program, it is first important to go over what this disorder is and what the possible market opportunity for the company could end up being.

Focal segmental glomerulosclerosis [FSGS] is a type of rare kidney disorder characterized as the formation of scar tissue in the glomeruli [small structures that filter out waste in the kidney]. What's the problem if this happens? With scarring coming about, along with inflammation, kidney functions are severely impacted. If not properly treated, then it could lead to kidney failure. Two such treatment options that might be possible would be dialysis or kidney transplant. There are several symptoms that these patients have to deal with, which could be one of the following:

  • Edema [swelling] in certain parts of the body like ankles or legs
  • Unexplained weight gain
  • Proteinuria [protein in the urine].

What makes it devastating is that it can arise with several causes. There is a genetic variation that is passed down from parents. Along with the formation of it due to toxic drug substances/medicines or from no known cause at all. The global focal segmental glomerulosclerosis market is expected to reach $51.72 billion by 2032. This is a large market opportunity and with the company potentially being on the cusp to provide superior proteinuria reduction compared to current 1st-line irbesartan, this is a good target for it.

With the symptoms I noted above, there is one in particular that relates to the efficacy endpoints established in both the phase 2 DUET and phase 3 DUPLEX studies. In both of these studies Travere was able to show that Filspari was able to reduce proteinuria by a considerable amount, especially compared to that of irbesartan. This particular drug is first-line standard of care [SOC] for the treatment of these patients with FSGS. Consider that this was developed with the mechanism of action [MOA] of blocking the angiotensin II receptor pathway specifically to reduce proteinuria seen in these patients. Where does this leave Filspari? Its mode of action is not only to act as an angiotensin II receptor blocker, but it also targets endothelin A receptor. The hope is that with this drug being able to block both of these receptor pathways, it is able to reduce proteinuria in a superior fashion compared to that of irbesartan. Travere's drug Filspari achieved such superiority compared to that of irbesartan in terms of proteinuria reduction in both children and adults in the DUET and DUPLEX studies.

The beauty of the company is that it ran the phase 3 DUPLEX study and the caliber of this goes beyond that of only showing that its drug Filspari was able to do well in terms of proteinuria reduction for the treatment of patients with focal segmental glomerulosclerosis [FSGS]. Why is that? Well, that's because it was the largest interventional study achieved showing that this drug was able to perform in a superior fashion compared to that than maximally dosed active comparator drug irbesartan. A total of 371 patients with primary and genetic FSGS were enrolled into this DUPLEX study and randomized 1:1 to receive either of the following doses:

  • 400 mg/day of FALSPARI titrated in week 2 and beyond to 800 mg/day as tolerated.
  • 150 mg/day of irbesartan titrated in week 2 and beyond to 300 mg/day as tolerated.

In terms of proteinuria in FSGS, this is where its drug shined well in. Consider that a pre-specified interim analysis of FSGS partial remission of proteinuria [FPRE] was achieved in a statistically significant manner of drug versus placebo. This endpoint over a 36-week period was shown to be achieved in 42% of patients given Filspari, compared to only 26% of those given active comparator irbesartan. The efficacy endpoint not achieved, though, was that of eGFR slope from day 1 to week 108.

The thing is that there is a major catalyst relating to this specific program and this would be the PDUFA target action date of January 13, 2026. This is the date by which the FDA will have by where it will decide whether or not Filspari should be approve for the treatment of patients with FSGS. A positive development for this program is that the company was notified that an advisory committee meeting is no longer necessary.

Financials

According to the 10-Q SEC Filing, Travere Therapeutics had cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities of $319.5 million as of June 30, 2025. A big reason on why it has the chat that it does is because of a public offering it enacted in November of 2024 to raise cash. That is it had an underwritten public offering of 9 million shares at an offering price of $134.7 million. The truth is that this company doesn't have a lot of cash on hand. Considering that it has a PDUFA target action date rapidly approaching, it will likely need to raise additional cash in the next several months.

This is based on what it notes in its very own 10-Q SEC filing, which is that it believes it only has enough cash on hand for a cash runway to fund its operations for at least the next 12 months. It does have a financial instrument in place if it chooses to utilize it. This would be with respect to an at-the-market equity offering agreement. Such an ATM agreement was established with Jefferies LLC, whereby it could offer and sell, from time to time up to $100 million of its common stock. To date no shares have been sold under this agreement, but this is an option that it has in place if it needs to raise cash immediately over the next several months.

The cash burn per quarter is approximately $125.6 million. This is broken down into $49.4 million in R&D expenses and then $76.2 million in SG&A expenses.

Risks To Business

There are several risks that investors should be aware of before investing in Travere Therapeutics. The first risk to consider would be in terms of the upcoming PDUFA target action date of January 13, 2026. Even though Filspari has been approved to treat patients with IgAN, there is no assurance that it will be approved under the sNDA to treat patients with FSGS. In addition, while the drug was shown to do well in terms of proteinuria reduction compared to that of current SOC irbesartan, it did not meet the endpoint of eGFR total slope from Day 1 to week 108. Thus, the risk here is that there is no assurance that proteinuria reduction being achieved alone is going to enough to warrant U.S. marketing approval of Filspari for these FSGS patients.

The second risk to consider would be in terms of the massive revenue spike seen in the most recent second quarter of 2025. It was revealed that net product revenues of Filspari reached $71.9 million during this most recent quarter, which ended up being a year-over-year revenue gain of 165%. The risk here is that upon the release of the next set of earnings from the company, there is no guarantee that the revenue to be generated will be similar to or superior to that which had already been reported. In essence, there is no assurance that the percentage growth rate in revenues is going to be this high.

The third and final risk to consider would be in terms of the financial position that this company finds itself in. The reason why I state that is because of what it had noted in terms of cash projection runway. As I stated above, it believes that it has enough cash on hand to fund its operations for at least the next 12 months. The risk here is that it may need to dilute existing shareholders in order to raise a considerable amount of cash to both fund its pipeline and to run its clinical studies. There is no assurance that it will be able to raise cash on considerable terms or to raise cash through its ATM agreement that it has in place.

Conclusion

Travere Therapeutics is in good shape for the long-term because it has already been able to receive U.S. marketing approval of its drug Filspari for the treatment of patients with IgAN. Where a milestone or catalyst opportunity exists is in terms of the upcoming expected FDA review of this drug for the treatment of patients with FSGS. The reason for such a review being possible is because of the sNDA that the company filed with the FDA. Not only was the sNDA of Filspari accepted by this U.S. agency but a PDUFA target action date of January 13, 2026 was set. The importance of this is that if the FDA approves this drug, it would be the first kind ever for the treatment of these patients with FSGS. Even if the FDA doesn't approve Filspari for FSGS immediately, I believe that not all would be lost.

First of all, it honestly depends upon the reason for the rejection. If it one minor CMC issue or something that can be fixed without requiring another large trial, then it will be easy to recover from such an issue. Secondly, the drug is already performing well in terms of revenue just on the IgAN indication alone. Why do I make such a claim? The reason why is because U.S. net product sales of Filspari grew by 165% year-over-year to $71.9 million during Q2 of 2025. If such a revenue growth trend continues then it will be good regardless of what happens with the upcoming PDUFA review date of Filspari for FSGS. With massive growth observed from Filspari in treating patients with IgAN, plus the potential to expand the market for this specific drug to target that of FSGS patients, I believe that it is important to set a Buy rating on this stock.

data.title: The Fed Won't Fix This
data.description:
All eyes will be on the Federal Reserve next week. The current consensus is overwhelmingly looking for a 25 bps reduction in the Fed Funds rate at the conclusion of the FOMC meeting.

Any possibility of a 50 bps cut probably went up in smoke after the hotter-than-expected August CPI reading earlier this week.

But not to worry, Morgan Stanley now projects the Federal Reserve will cut rates by 25 bps at each of their next four FOMC meetings. Unfortunately, the announcement of the rate cut at the end of the Fed's meeting next week feels like the classic 'buy the rumor, sell the news' type moment.

The markets have continued to rally despite a horrid series of jobs reports and downward job revisions over the past several weeks. Equities are priced at extreme levels using a variety of traditional valuation metrics like the 'Buffett Indicator' or market cap to GDP ratio. Stocks are also trading very dearly, looking at S&P 500 price-to-sales ratios, Shiller P/E, or NASDAQ price-to-sales ratios.

As importantly, the credit markets have also front-run this upcoming reduction in the Fed Funds rate. The yield on the 10-Year Treasury is down below 4.1% after being as high as 4.8% earlier in the year. Average mortgage rates have also come down to their lowest levels since last October.

In addition, a 25 bps, 50 bps, or even 100 bps in rate cuts in the coming quarters are only going to be of marginal help to the U.S. economy, although they should marginally lower debt service on the massive federal debt, which has blown through $37 trillion and continues to march higher at an alarming rate.

Lower mortgage rates will only marginally help what has become an increasingly toxic housing sector. For the first time since records were kept, the average cost of a new home was below the average price of an existing home in both June (6.5% discount) and July (4% discount). Of note, this does not capture the large incentives (mortgage buy-downs, help with closing costs, free upgrades, etc.) home builders are needing to employ to move inventory. Lennar (LEN) had just over $50,000 per home in incentives in Q2 with an average sales price of $389,000. Mortgage rates coming down to 5.5% to 6%, if that happens, will do little to revise the housing sector, although it could marginally lower the incentive costs for home builders.

Then we have commercial real estate, or CRE. As I just noted, the fundamentals for both the multifamily and office sectors continue to deteriorate. These two charts of CRE show that approximately $3.5 trillion of debt obligations will need to be refinanced at higher rates in coming quarters and years. A few interest rate reductions are not going to meaningfully improve market dynamics here, especially if the economy heads into recession in 2026.

Nor will slightly lower interest rates help consumers in a measurable way. The job market, as noted, is deteriorating. This is starting to impact consumer confidence. Consumer sentiment readings just fell from 58.2 to 55.4, and under the 58 consensus. This trend is likely to continue as long as the jobs market remains weak. We still also don't know what the impact of the AI revolution will be in the coming quarters. I recently penned a piece exploring whether AI will trigger a White-Collar Recession.

A slight decrease in interest costs will do little to help the situation with student loans, where delinquency rates are surging after payments have recently resumed after nearly a four-year taxpayer-financed hiatus. Over 40 million Americans have student loan balances. At the current and rising delinquency rate, millions will soon face wage garnishments and confiscation of any tax refunds in 2026.

A 25 bps point reduction in interest rates will also do little on the credit card front with a record $1.21 trillion in balances. And credit card delinquency rates are surging. Serious delinquent accounts reach seven percent in Q1, just one percent below that of the peak in the Great Financial Crisis.

A downtick in rates will also be of marginal benefit to auto loans, which now total a record $1.66 trillion. In 2025, the average new vehicle price has risen to nearly $50,000. Almost 20% of buyers are locked into $1,000 monthly payments, and one in five new auto loans in 2025 has seven-year terms. Auto repossessions have already hit their highest levels since 2009. Subprime auto loan delinquencies have moved over five percent, above their 2008 peak.

In summary, next week's financial headlines will be intently focused on actions and commentary at the conclusion of the FOMC meeting. It is highly likely that investors will see the first cut to the Fed Funds rate since September of last year. However, a 25 bps cut or even a series of them will not help the struggling jobs market. In addition, they will only be of marginal assistance in the consumer and real estate markets. Finally, the FOMC announcement is unlikely to power the next leg of a rally in a market that is clearly overbought, as the central bank can't fix what currently ails the U.S. economy.

data.title: INTF: International ETF With An Edge Over The Benchmark
data.description:
INTF strategy

iShares International Equity Factor ETF (NYSEARCA:INTF) was launched on 04/28/2015 and tracks the STOXX International Equity Factor Index. INTF has 458 holdings, a 30-day SEC yield of 2.67% and an expense ratio of 0.16%. Distributions are paid semi-annually.

As described in the prospectus by iShares, the methodology starts from the STOXX Global 1800 ex USA index, which measures the performance of the largest companies of the developed equity market excluding the U.S.

Stocks are selected and weighted using an optimization process based on five factors: momentum, quality, value, low volatility and size. The momentum score is calculated from price momentum, earnings momentum and earnings announcement drift. The quality score is calculated from profitability, share dilution, accruals, changes in net operating assets and ESG factors. The value score is calculated from book value-to-price, dividend yield, earnings yield and cash flow yield.

The portfolio is rebalanced quarterly with maximum limits of 10% in any constituent, 22.5% in aggregate in those weighing more than 4.5%, and some constraints on country and sector exposure. The turnover rate in the most recent fiscal year was 22%. This article will use as a benchmark an international index excluding the U.S. and Canada, represented by iShares MSCI EAFE ETF (EFA).

The INTF portfolio

The portfolio has about 75% of asset value in large and mega-cap companies, with a focus on Japan (23.9%) and significant exposure in the UK (12.1%). Other countries are below 9%. The next chart lists the top 15 countries, representing 95.2% of assets. Unlike EFA, INTF includes Canada.

The portfolio has a focus on financials (26.7%), and significant exposure in industrials (18.3%) and consumer discretionary (12%). Other sectors are below 8%. Like the geographical allocation, the sector breakdown of INTF isn’t much different from the benchmark, although it moderately downplays healthcare and communication.

The portfolio is well-diversified and risks related to individual companies are low. Indeed the top 10 holdings, listed in the next table, represent only 10.8% of asset value and the heaviest position weighs 1.93%.
